page: Microsoft Azure HB176rs_v4 is a high-performance compute instance belonging to the HBv4 server family. It features 176 dedicated vCPUs built on an x86_64 Intel processor architecture. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 4.36 GB of RAM per core. Virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

This server configuration includes 4332 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage options. For high-speed interconnectivity and low-latency networking, the instance is RDMA (Remote Direct Memory Access) capable. It does not include any dedicated graphics processing units (GPUs) and does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the HB176rs_v4 offers a high density of dedicated compute resources and substantial local storage capacity, making it suitable for large-scale parallel workloads without the overhead of GPU hardware. The combination of RDMA capability, high core count, and balanced memory-to-core ratio makes this instance well-suited for high-performance computing (HPC) applications, large-scale simulations, computational fluid dynamics, and intensive database or storage-bound workloads.
description: The Microsoft Azure HB176rs_v4 is an x86_64 architecture server within the HBv4 family, designed for high-performance compute workloads. It features 176 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor and is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of RAM, yielding 4.36 GB of memory per core, and includes 4332 GB of local storage with Premium Storage compatibility. To support clustered and distributed environments, the server is RDMA-capable, enabling low-latency networking. It does not feature any integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This hardware profile is optimized for compute-heavy and storage-intensive tasks, making it suitable for high-performance computing, large-scale simulations, and complex database operations.

tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring dedicated processors, massive local storage, and low-latency RDMA networking for parallel processing workloads.
